title: cassia biflora var. semperflorens
intro: Cassia biflora var. semperflorens, also known as the Ever-Blooming Cassia, is a flowering shrub native to tropical regions, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The medicinal properties of Cassia biflora var. semperflorens have been shown to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, digestive relief, and immune system support, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
